name: Bachelor of Architecture

dimensions.name: Curriculum coherence & progression
dimensions.justification: This flagship first-professional (five-year, 150-credit) plan has an exemplary progression: the studio spine (ARCH 195 -> 196 -> 295 -> 296 -> 395 -> 396 -> 463/495 -> 561/595) advances in lockstep with parallel technical sequences (Construction I/II ARCH 223/224, Structures I/II ARCH 303/304, Environmental Control Systems I/II ARCH 309/314) and History I/II (ARCH 210/211), so representation, tectonics, and theory mature together term over term. Prerequisite depths rise smoothly from 0 to 7 with no abrupt jumps.
dimensions.suggestion: Explicitly designate the fourth/fifth-year Options and Advanced studios as an 'integrated/comprehensive' studio and state which technical courses (structures, ECS) are synthesized there, so the culminating integration is legible on the page.

dimensions.name: Prerequisite structure health
dimensions.justification: The prerequisite graph is deep and sensible (deepestPrereqChain 7) and tracks the studio spine cleanly through ARCH 561 Synthesis Seminar (depth 7). Only 3 dangling references exist (missingCoursesSample: GER 200, GER 300, PHYS 102A), and all three are general-education placeholders and a physics-lab co-requisite rather than genuine architecture breaks.
dimensions.suggestion: Register the PHYS 102A General Physics I Lab entry and resolve the GER 200/300 placeholders so the metrics show zero dangling references.

dimensions.name: Learning outcomes & objectives
dimensions.justification: No program-level learning outcomes or educational objectives appear anywhere in the provided data; only the plan of study and course description excerpts are present. For a NAAB-accredited first-professional degree this absence is a real catalog finding.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish explicit program learning outcomes mapped to the NAAB Student Criteria (design synthesis, technical integration, professional practice, history/theory), which also supports accreditation reporting.

dimensions.name: Credit distribution & balance
dimensions.justification: The plan reconciles exactly: summedTermCredits 150 equals statedTotalCredits 150 with creditReconcileDelta 0.0, and terms are evenly loaded at 14-16 credits across ten semesters. The split is transparent, balancing a deep studio/technical major core, a gen-ed block (ENGL 101/102, MATH 105/107, PHYS 102), and distributed ARCH electives labeled by category (Technology, History/Theory).
dimensions.suggestion: Add a one-line credit-summary table (studio vs. technical core vs. history/theory vs. gen-ed vs. electives) so the balanced distribution is visible without reading all ten terms.

dimensions.name: Presentation & clarity
dimensions.justification: Terms are clearly labeled with per-term credits and most courses carry substantive descriptions. Legibility is reduced by parsing artifacts: placeholder rows such as 'or ARCH 566 or Advanced Architectural Design Studio' with null credits, repeated 'ARCH Elective iii' labels, and description excerpts where catalog boilerplate ('ARCH 2**. Architecture Elective. 3 credits, 3 contact hours (3;0;0)') has leaked into ARCH 196/296/396.
dimensions.suggestion: Render alternative-course rows (ARCH 464 'or ARCH 566') as single entries and strip the leaked contact-hour boilerplate from the continuation-studio descriptions.

dimensions.name: Industry & currency relevance
dimensions.justification: The required core reflects durable professional practice well (Construction I/II, Structures I/II, ECS I/II passive+active, Professional Practice I/II ARCH 472/475, and a Synthesis Seminar). However digital and computational fluency is thin in the required spine: the only computing requirement is the dated CS 104 'Computer Programming and Graphics Problems,' while BIM (ARCH 337), digital fabrication (ARCH 301/335), and building-performance simulation sit only in electives, and sustainability lives largely inside ECS.
dimensions.suggestion: Make BIM (ARCH 337) and a building-performance/energy-modeling course required rather than elective, and modernize or replace CS 104 with a computational-design course.

descriptionQuality.justification: Core descriptions are specific and informative (ARCH 210/211 History, ARCH 223/224 Construction, ARCH 303/304 Structures, ARCH 309/314 ECS all give genuine topic content). Quality is dragged down by the continuation studios (ARCH 196 'A continuation of ARCH 195. ARCH 2**...', ARCH 296, ARCH 395 'This course is a continuation of ARCH 296.') that are thin or carry leaked catalog boilerplate.

prerequisiteClarity.justification: Most prerequisites are explicit and consistent, but several compound raw strings mix 'and'/'or' without parentheses so precedence is ambiguous (e.g., ARCH 464 'ARCH 396 or ARCH 364 and ARCH 304 or ARCH 329 and ARCH 314 or ARCH 327 and ARCH 324' and ARCH 211 'ARCH 210 or ARCH 251 and ARCH 252'). Dangling GER/PHYS-lab references are minor.

missingTopics.topic: Building codes, life-safety, and accessibility/universal design
missingTopics.rationale: Professional Practice I/II (ARCH 472/475) cover project delivery and firm structure but no course explicitly teaches code compliance, egress/life-safety, or ADA/universal design, which are licensure (ARE) and practice essentials.

ECS I/II (ARCH 309/314) teach passive and active systems conceptually, but the plan has no course on quantitative energy/daylight modeling with contemporary simulation tools, now standard in high-performance design.

BIM (ARCH 337) and digital fabrication/tectonics (ARCH 301/335) exist only as electives while the sole required computing course is the dated CS 104; parametric modeling (Rhino/Grasshopper) is absent from the required spine.
Construction I/II (ARCH 223/224) survey wood/steel/masonry/concrete assemblies without addressing embodied carbon or whole-life LCA, now central to responsible materials selection.

missingTopics.topic: Construction cost, estimating, and project delivery methods
missingTopics.rationale: The professional-practice sequence omits construction economics, estimating, and modern delivery (design-build, IPD), leaving a business-of-building gap for graduates entering practice.

emergingTrends.rationale: Text-to-image and ML-driven design exploration are reshaping early-stage studio work; ARCH 110/156 Tools and Techniques and the studio sequence are the natural place to introduce responsible generative-AI workflows, replacing the aging CS 104 emphasis.

emergingTrends.trend: Decarbonization and net-zero / whole-life carbon design
emergingTrends.rationale: Codes and clients increasingly demand net-zero and embodied-carbon accounting; the ECS sequence (ARCH 309/314) and Construction courses should evolve toward carbon-quantified design.

emergingTrends.trend: BIM-based integrated delivery and digital twins
emergingTrends.rationale: Industry practice now assumes Revit-level BIM fluency and lifecycle digital twins; elevating ARCH 337 from elective to requirement positions graduates for contemporary coordinated project delivery.
Robotic assembly and CNC/3D-printed construction are moving from research to practice; ARCH 301/335 digital-fabrication content could seed a required maker/robotics component.

emergingTrends.trend: Mass timber and low-carbon structural systems
emergingTrends.rationale: Cross-laminated timber and hybrid systems are transforming mid-rise construction; Structures I/II (ARCH 303/304) and Construction I/II should add mass-timber design and detailing.

topSuggestions: Publish explicit program learning outcomes mapped to the NAAB Student Criteria; they are absent from the catalog data and required for accreditation.
Require BIM (ARCH 337) and a building-performance/energy-simulation course, and modernize or replace the dated CS 104 with computational-design content.
Add a codes/life-safety/accessibility course and construction-cost/project-delivery content to close the biggest licensure and business-of-building gaps.
Integrate embodied-carbon/LCA and mass-timber design into Construction I/II (ARCH 223/224) and Structures I/II (ARCH 303/304).
Clean plan-of-study artifacts (the 'or ARCH 566' null-credit row, repeated 'ARCH Elective iii' labels, leaked contact-hour boilerplate) and resolve the 3 dangling references (GER 200/300, PHYS 102A).
